title: Postman の 422 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article provides accurate and well-explained information regarding the 422 Unprocessable Entity error in the context of Postman, GitHub API, and FastAPI.
Most factual claims are supported by multiple reputable sources, including official documentation, company blogs, and community discussions.
The step-by-step solutions for common causes are practical and align with best practices.
There is a minor mismatch in the interpretation of one external URL in the Editor's Note.

sources.title: HTTP 422 Error: How to Diagnose and Fix Unprocessable Entity Responses - Postman Blog
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity status code indicates that while your API request was well-formed and syntactically valid, the server couldn't process it due to semantic or business logic errors in the request body. Common causes include missing required fields, incorrect data types, invalid formats, or constraint violations. Incorrect Content-Type headers can also cause 422 errors. The Postman Console can be used to inspect request headers and body for debugging.

sources.title: What is HTTP 422 Error? (Unprocessable Entity) - Scrapfly Blog
sources.claim: HTTP error 422 Unprocessable Entity occurs when the server understands the request but finds that the content is syntactically correct, yet semantically invalid. Common causes include missing required fields, invalid data formats, or values that don't meet server validation rules.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Entity Explained - KeyCDN Support
sources.claim: A 422 status code occurs when a request is well-formed, however, due to semantic errors it is unable to be processed. This HTTP status was introduced in RFC 4918. The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code means the server understands the content type of the request entity and the syntax of the request entity is correct but was unable to process the contained instructions.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Content - HTTP - MDN Web Docs
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 Unprocessable Content client error response status code indicates that the server understood the content type of the request content, and the syntax of the request content was correct, but it was unable to process the contained instructions. Clients that receive a 422 response should expect that repeating the request without modification will fail with the same error.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Content - HTTP status code explained
sources.claim: The 422 Unprocessable Content error indicates the server understands the request but finds the content semantically invalid. The code was previously named "Unprocessable Entity" before being renamed to "Unprocessable Content". Common triggers include missing required fields, wrong data types, invalid email or date formats, duplicate unique values, and enum values outside the allowed set. Confirm the Content-Type header matches the body format.

sources.title: What is a 422 Status Code? - Kinex Media
sources.claim: A 422 Unprocessable Entity HTTP status code indicates that the server understands the format of the incoming data (e.g., JSON, XML) but cannot process its content due to semantic errors.

sources.title: 422エラー（Unprocessable Entity）とは？意味をわかりやすく解説 - SEOタイムズ
sources.claim: 422エラー（Unprocessable Entity）は、HTTPプロトコルの一部であるWebDAVを利用して、サーバーに対するリクエストの構文は正しいものの、そのリクエスト内容がサーバーで処理できない場合に返されるステータスコードです。具体的には、クライアントから送信されたデータが形式としては正しいものの、意味的に不適切な場合に発生します。主な原因は、必須フィールドの欠落、不正なデータ形式、APIリクエストの不適切な内容です。

sources.title: 400 Bad Request vs 422 Unprocessable Entity - Beeceptor
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity status code means that while the server was able to interpret the request sent, it is still not able to process it due to some logical error. This is used when the request syntax is correct, but the data fails validation or business rules.

sources.title: HTTP 422エラーの解決方法 - Kinsta
sources.claim: 422エラーは、サーバー側がリクエストを理解しているにもかかわらず、処理ができないというHTTPステータスコードで、正式名称は「422 Unprocessable Entity（処理できないエンティティ）」です。

sources.title: Http error: 422 - Unprocessable Entity - Productive Shop
sources.claim: The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code means that the server cannot understand the content of the request. This is generally caused by incorrect syntax in the request. For example, a common cause of this error is when someone tries to submit a form with invalid data.

sources.title: Error: Cache Service Responded with 422 - Postman Blog
sources.claim: HTTP 422 errors occur when your request is syntactically correct but contains invalid data. It means the request reached the server successfully, but it couldn't be processed due to semantic issues with your data, such as type mismatches (sending strings where numbers are required) or incorrect Content-Type headers.

sources.title: Postmanでの422エラーの解決方法 - Apidog
sources.claim: 422 Unprocessable Entityエラーは、HTTP/1.1仕様の一部であり、RESTful APIで頻繁に発生します。このエラーは通常、リクエストが文法的に正しく、形式が整っているシナリオで発生しますが、リクエスト内のデータが必要な検証ルールやビジネスロジックを満たさない場合に発生します。サーバーレスポンスには、422エラーの原因や具体的なエラーメッセージが含まれている場合があります。

sources.title: GitHub API - 422: Unprocessable Entity: Review comments is invalid and Review threads is invalid · community · Discussion #42272 · GitHub
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 error in the GitHub REST API means "Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed." This typically occurs when the parameters provided in the request are invalid or do not meet the requirements of the API endpoint. Troubleshooting involves reviewing endpoint documentation, checking request body format, validating input data types and constraints, and confirming authentication/permissions.

sources.title: Troubleshooting the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: If you omit required parameters or you use the wrong type for a parameter, you may receive a 422 Unprocessable Entity response and an "Invalid request" error message from the GitHub API. The response body will include an `errors` property, which includes a `code` property to help you diagnose the problem. This can be caused by a resource not existing, a required parameter not being specified, or invalid formatting of a parameter.

sources.title: "Create a reference" API call fails with HTTP 422 without meaningful message · Issue #4887 · github/rest-api-description
sources.claim: HTTP 422 (Unprocessable Entity) typically occurs when the server understands the request but cannot process it due to semantic errors when using GitHub's REST API. This could be due to invalid or missing data in your request. Checking the request body, validating input data types and constraints, and referring to error message details are troubleshooting steps.

sources.title: POST request to FastAPI endpoint produces error 422: "value is not a valid dict" | Sentry
sources.claim: A POST request to a FastAPI endpoint can fail with a 422 error and the detail `{"loc": ["body"], "msg": "value is not a valid dict", "type": "type_error.dict"}` if the client sends a list instead of a dictionary, or generally if the payload does not match the expected Pydantic model.

sources.title: Value is not a valid dict when posting JSON data through Postman to FastAPI backend
sources.claim: The 422 Unprocessable Entity error with the message "value is not a valid dict" shows that the body received by FastAPI doesn't match the expected format. This often occurs when sending data that is not a dictionary when one is expected by a Pydantic model. In Postman, ensure to go to Body -> raw and select JSON from the dropdown list.

sources.title: POST request response 422 error {'detail': [{'loc': ['body'], 'msg': 'value is not a valid dict', 'type': 'type_error.dict'}]} - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: When sending JSON data to FastAPI, if the Pydantic model expects a dictionary but receives a list, a 422 error with `"msg": "value is not a valid dict"` can occur. This highlights a type mismatch.

sources.title: Rename 422 from "Unprocessable Entity" to "Unprocessable Content" · Issue #664 · hyperium/http - GitHub
sources.claim: RFC 9110, an Internet Standard from June 2022, renames the 422 status code from "Unprocessable Entity" to "Unprocessable Content", but its definition remains the same: the server understands the content type and syntax, but cannot process the contained instructions due to semantic errors.

sources.title: POSTMAN Error 422 - Unprocessable Entity, with GitHub API. What is it and how do solve it?
sources.claim: On Stack Overflow, a solution to a Postman 422 error with GitHub API was to change the `Content-Type` header from `text/plain` to `application/json`.

sources.title: Okta API Error Codes - Okta Developer
sources.claim: API documentation for specific endpoints should be consulted to understand preconditions and expected parameters, as validation failures for these can lead to errors.

sources.title: 422エラー（Unprocessable Entity）とは？原因と解決方法をわかりやすく解説
sources.claim: When developing and operating web applications, various HTTP errors are encountered, and 422 errors often occur during form submission or API integration. The error indicates that the server understood the request syntax but cannot process its content. Checking the error response, confirming data, and cross-referencing with documentation are key steps for resolution. FastAPI and other frameworks often return detailed error messages in a 'detail' field.

sources.title: Manual Test Passing but failing in Collection Runner - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: When running a collection in Postman, if a request involves file uploads via `form-data`, the files must be placed in the Postman working directory for the collection runner to locate them, otherwise a 422 error can occur. There is also an option 'Allow reading files outside working directory' in Postman settings.

sources.title: Postman POST request works but running a collection on the same request returns 422 error
sources.claim: Files uploaded through form-data must be placed in the Postman working directory when running a collection. Otherwise, the collection runner won't locate them and will send a request with no files attached, resulting in a 422 error. This differs from executing individual requests.

sources.title: 422: "value is not a valid dict" on Form() BaseModel validation - @app.post uses Path(), File() and Form() - Request called from SwaggerUI · Issue #5349 - GitHub
sources.claim: When using FastAPI with Pydantic models for request bodies, if the incoming data doesn't conform to the expected dictionary structure or types, a 422 Unprocessable Entity error with `"loc": ["body", "field_name"], "msg": "value is not a valid dict"` is returned.

sources.title: 422 error. : r/FastAPI - Reddit
sources.claim: FastAPI's 422 errors typically mean that the data received by the API does not meet what it expects, often due to an invalid JSON structure or type mismatches. The response content provides details like `"loc": ["body"], "msg": "value is not a valid dict"`.

sources.title: Couldn't upload file in Postman Web · Issue #9361 - GitHub
sources.claim: When attempting to select a file for a POST `multipart/form-data` request in Postman Web, an error "Couldn't upload file. Make sure that Postman can read files inside the working directory" can occur. This indicates an issue with Postman's access to the file path.

sources.title: fix postman error: Couldn't upload file - YouTube
sources.claim: To resolve the "Couldn't upload file. Make sure that Postman can read files inside the working directory" error for `form-data` file uploads, ensure the 'Allow reading files outside working directory' setting is enabled in Postman.

sources.title: Postman Request Body: JSON, Form-data, URL-encoded & Binary Explained - YouTube
sources.claim: `form-data` is often used for sending multipart data, like when uploading a file, and supports both text and file uploads. When `form-data` is selected, the `Content-Type` header changes automatically, but sometimes needs double-checking.

sources.title: FastAPI 422 Unprocessable Entity when using with POST Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: In FastAPI, if an endpoint expects a Pydantic model in a JSON body but receives data from `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` or from query parameters instead of the body, a 422 error can occur due to a type mismatch or incorrect parameter location.

sources.title: 422 - Validation Failed · Issue #149 · prontolabs/pronto - GitHub
sources.claim: GitHub API responses for 422 'Validation Failed' errors often include `resource`, `code`, and `field` in the error summary.

sources.title: validation error responses in REST API - Software Engineering Stack Exchange
sources.claim: REST APIs often return validation error messages in an array format, where each element can include field name, error code, and error message, allowing for details on multiple validation issues.

sources.title: Common API Errors & How to Fix Them - TechnologyAdvice
sources.claim: When an API failure occurs, a numeric error message is sent back that attempts to identify what error was committed to the user. Always refer to the API documentation to validate the expected HTTP methods, parameters, and headers.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Entity (WebDAV) (RFC 4918) The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic errors. · community · Discussion #72695 - GitHub
sources.claim: The GitHub REST API can return a 422 Unprocessable Entity error if trying to create a Git reference (branch or tag) that already exists, indicating a semantic error despite a well-formed request.

citation_mismatches.claimed: 422エラーの原因が検索クエリの不正さに限定されるとされています
citation_mismatches.actual: The accepted answer states that changing the `Content-Type` from `text/plain` to `application/json` fixed the issue, suggesting the cause was a `Content-Type` mismatch rather than exclusively invalid search queries. While some comments mention invalid query parameters, the primary resolution provided focuses on the header.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Correct the statement in the Editor's Note regarding the Stack Overflow post about GitHub API's 422 error to accurately reflect the content of the linked page.
Optional improvement: Add links to official documentation or primary sources for key claims.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
